title: Febrile Infant (≤ 56 Days Old)
warning: Neonatal fever in infants ≤ 28 days is a medical emergency. Do not delay IV antibiotics.

start.title: Age-Based Risk Stratification
start.prompt: Identify patient's exact age in days:

age_21.title: Full Sepsis Workup & Admission
age_21.prompt: Perform full sepsis workup including blood culture, urine culture via catheter, lumbar puncture (CSF culture/gram stain/PCR), and inflammatory markers (CRP/Procalcitonin). Admit for IV Ampicillin + Ceftazidime (or Gentamicin) immediately.
actions: Perform Lumbar Puncture (LP) for CSF analysis
Initiate IV Ampicillin 50 mg/kg + IV Ceftazidime 50 mg/kg (or Gentamicin 2.5 mg/kg)

age_22_28.title: Inflammatory Marker Risk Stratification
age_22_28.prompt: Obtain CBC, blood culture, catheter urine UA/culture, and inflammatory markers (Procalcitonin, CRP, ANC). Are ALL inflammatory markers and urine UA completely normal?

age_22_28_low.title: Admission & Close Observation
age_22_28_low.prompt: Lumbar puncture is optional. Admit for observation and track blood/urine cultures. IV antibiotics may be deferred if CSF not obtained.
actions: Admit for observation and culture monitoring
Decline or defer lumbar puncture if family/clinician agree

full_sepsis_workup.title: Escalated Workup & IV Antibiotics
full_sepsis_workup.prompt: Perform Lumbar Puncture (LP) for CSF. Administer empiric IV Ceftriaxone (or Ampicillin + Ceftazidime) and admit.

age_29_56.title: Low-Risk Protocol Criteria
age_29_56.prompt: Is the infant well-appearing, normal UA, ANC < 4000, CRP < 20, Procalcitonin < 0.5?

home_discharge.title: Safe Discharge with 24h Follow-up
home_discharge.prompt: May discharge home without antibiotics. Ensure family is reliable, has transportation, and is scheduled for clinical recheck/pediatrician visit within 24 hours.
actions: Counsel family on strict return criteria

title: ED Sepsis & Septic Shock
warning: Sepsis recognition is time-critical. Initiate treatment bundle within 60 minutes for severe shock.

start.title: Shock Classification
start.prompt: Evaluate for signs of shock (prolonged capillary refill >2s, cold extremities, altered mental status, weak pulses, or hypotension):

options.label: Yellow Zone (Compensated Shock / Tachycardia & Normal BP)

red_zone.title: Aggressive Resuscitation (Red Zone)
red_zone.prompt: Secure IV/IO access within 5 minutes. Administer 20 mL/kg Isotonic Crystalloid bolus over 5-10 minutes. Draw blood culture and CBC. Give empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics (Ceftriaxone + Vancomycin) within 60 minutes. Initiate vasoactive drugs (Norepinephrine or Epinephrine) if shock persists after 40-60 mL/kg fluids.
actions: Administer 20 mL/kg Normal Saline or Lactated Ringer's bolus over 5-10 mins
Administer IV Ceftriaxone 75 mg/kg + IV Vancomycin 15 mg/kg within 60 minutes
Initiate Epinephrine or Norepinephrine infusion for fluid-refractory shock

yellow_zone.title: Targeted Evaluation (Yellow Zone)
yellow_zone.prompt: Establish IV access. Administer 20 mL/kg Isotonic Crystalloid bolus over 20-30 minutes. Send blood culture, CBC, lactate, and CRP. Administer antibiotics within 180 minutes.
actions: Administer 20 mL/kg crystalloid bolus over 20 mins
Obtain blood culture, CBC, CMP, venous lactate
Administer broad-spectrum antibiotics IV within 180 mins

green_zone.title: Standard Evaluation (Green Zone)
green_zone.prompt: Evaluate for localized source of fever (otitis, pharyngitis, UTI, pneumonia). Manage symptoms. Observe and reassess.

title: Status Epilepticus
warning: Continuous seizure activity >= 5 minutes is Status Epilepticus. Timed escalations are critical to prevent neuronal injury.

start.title: Stage 1 (0–10 Minutes)
start.prompt: Active seizure >= 5 minutes. Administer first-line Benzodiazepines (IV Lorazepam or IV/IN/IM Midazolam). Did the seizure stop?

stage_2.title: Stage 2 (10–30 Minutes)
stage_2.prompt: Administer second dose of Benzodiazepines. Initiate second-line IV non-benzodiazepine anti-seizure medication (Levetiracetam/Keppra, Fosphenytoin, or Valproate). Did the seizure stop?

stage_3.title: Stage 3 (Refractory Status)
stage_3.prompt: Refractory Status Epilepticus. Prepare for rapid sequence intubation (RSI) using propofol/ketamine. Initiate continuous anesthetic infusion (Midazolam, Propofol, or Pentobarbital). Urgent ICU admission and continuous EEG monitoring required.
actions: Initiate continuous IV infusion of Midazolam (0.2-2.0 mg/kg/hr) or Propofol

post_seizure_obs.title: Post-Ictal Observation
post_seizure_obs.prompt: Seizure stopped. Place in lateral recovery position, support airway, check fingerstick glucose and electrolytes, monitor for post-ictal recovery.
actions: Observe until returned to baseline neuro status

title: Diabetic Ketoacidosis (DKA)
warning: Do not administer insulin bolus in pediatric DKA. Delay insulin infusion by 1-2 hours after initiating fluid resuscitation to minimize cerebral edema risk.

start.title: Diagnosis Verification
start.prompt: Verify DKA criteria: Blood Glucose > 200 mg/dL, Venous pH < 7.3, Bicarbonate < 15 mEq/L, and ketonuria/ketonemia. Assess mental status (GCS):

options.label: GCS < 14 or abnormal neurological signs (high risk cerebral edema)

dka_resuscitation.title: Fluid and Insulin Initiation
dka_resuscitation.prompt: 1. Administer 10-20 mL/kg Isotonic Crystalloid (NS) over 1-2 hours. 2. Establish two-bag IV fluid system (one containing Dextrose, one without; adjust dextrose based on blood glucose drop). 3. Initiate continuous IV Insulin infusion at 0.05 - 0.1 units/kg/hour delayed 1-2 hours after fluids start. Does blood glucose remain stable?

options.label: No (rapid glucose drop > 100 mg/dL/hr or hypokalemia)

critical_dka.title: Emergent ICU Admission & Brain Protection
critical_dka.prompt: High risk for cerebral edema. Admit directly to PICU. Initiate hyperosmolar therapy (Mannitol 0.5-1.0 g/kg IV or 3% Hypertonic Saline 3-5 mL/kg IV). Slow down fluid administration, secure airway under controlled conditions if GCS declines.
actions: Administer Mannitol 1 g/kg IV over 20 minutes or 3% Hypertonic Saline 3 mL/kg IV

dka_monitoring.title: DKA ICU/Ward Monitoring
dka_monitoring.prompt: Maintain fluid rate and insulin infusion. Check blood glucose hourly and electrolytes/venous blood gas every 2 hours. Monitor closely for headache, bradycardia, or pupillary changes.
actions: Every 2 hours BMP & Venous blood gas
Add Potassium (K-Phos and K-Acetate) to IV fluids once urine output is confirmed
Continuous ECG monitoring for T-wave changes

dka_adjustments.title: Fluid/Insulin Adjustments
dka_adjustments.prompt: Adjust the two-bag fluid ratio to increase dextrose concentration if blood glucose drops > 100 mg/dL/hr. Hold or reduce insulin infusion only if hypoglycemia is severe, or potassium drops < 3.3 mEq/L (add potassium to bag).
actions: Increase Dextrose to D10 or D12.5 in IV fluids
Administer potassium replacement if serum K < 3.3 mEq/L before starting/continuing insulin

title: Sickle Cell Disease with Fever
warning: Fever in sickle cell disease is an emergency due to functional asplenia and rapid pneumococcal sepsis. Administer Ceftriaxone within 60 minutes of presentation.

start.title: Triage & Initial Screening
start.prompt: Obtain quick vitals. Is patient showing signs of toxic appearance, hypotension, respiratory distress, or severe splenic enlargement?

options.label: Yes (toxic, respiratory distress, or severe splenomegaly)

standard_scd_fever.title: Rapid Workup & Antibiotics
standard_scd_fever.prompt: Secure IV access. Draw CBC, reticulocyte count, blood culture, and type & screen. Administer IV Ceftriaxone within 60 minutes. Obtain chest X-ray if cough or chest pain is present.
actions: Administer IV Ceftriaxone 50 mg/kg (Max 2g) within 60 minutes of triage
Order Chest X-ray if respiratory symptoms present
cantMiss: Acute Chest Syndrome (new infiltrate on CXR)

critical_scd.title: SCD Emergency Resuscitation
critical_scd.prompt: Immediate oxygen therapy, IV/IO access, fluid resuscitation (cautious 10 mL/kg bolus if hypovolemic), IV Ceftriaxone, and crossmatch PRBCs. Consult Hematology and prepare for admission.
actions: Order emergent CBC, reticulocyte count, and type & crossmatch 2 units PRBCs
Bedside ultrasound to check spleen size
cantMiss: Splenic Sequestration (acute drop in Hb + splenomegaly)
